title: archidendropsis paivana subsp. paivana
intro: Archidendropsis paivana subsp. paivana, also known as the Hawaiian hickory, is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Archidendropsis paivana subsp. paivana include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and bark are typically used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures.
